Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To act without wisdom and bring trouble upon oneself.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: To behave foolishly and suffer consequences.
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, family advice, moral correction
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects Dangme value of prudence in communal decision-making traditions in Ada.
First Example Sentence: O atsɛ kɛ he blɔ mli, ni kɛ lɛ he gbɛi.
Second Example Sentence: Mi be he ɔ, kɛ o atsɛ kɛ he blɔ mli.
Third Example Sentence: Ba ko atsɛ kɛ he blɔ mli kɛje he womi.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Clause with subject repetition for emphasis
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common in Ada elder discourse during community arbitration.
First Example Sentence: Ngɛ tsu mli he ngɛ, lɛ mi he je.
Second Example Sentence: O ko he, ngɛ tsu mli he ngɛ.
Third Example Sentence: Ni tsɛ, ngɛ tsu mli he ngɛ.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from Dangme fishing life where fortunes shift with tides.
First Example Sentence: Blɔ be he mli nyɛ, mi ko je kɛ lɛ.
Second Example Sentence: Ni kɛ tsu, blɔ be he mli nyɛ.
Third Example Sentence: O he ngɛ lɛ, blɔ be he mli nyɛ.

Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Wait carefully before making a decision.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to Dangme council deliberation customs.
First Example Sentence: He gbɛi kɛ lɛ tsɔ ni kɛ he blɔ.
Second Example Sentence: Mi he o, he gbɛi kɛ lɛ tsɔ.
Third Example Sentence: Ba he gbɛi kɛ lɛ tsɔ kɛje he womi.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used in Ada storytelling traditions when narrators unveil plot twists.

Second Example Sentence: Ko tsu lɛ he ɔtsɛ mli he womi.
Third Example Sentence: Ba he, o tsu lɛ he ɔtsɛ.
